findings.title: img missing alt attribute
findings.message: The new hero banner image has no alt attribute. Screen reader users will hear the file name (`banner.svg`) or nothing useful at all. Add `alt="Validis"` if the banner is informative, or `alt=""` if it's purely decorative and the surrounding context already conveys the information.

findings.title: Icon-only search button has no accessible name
findings.message: This button contains only the `pi-search` icon and no visible text. Screen reader users hear nothing about its purpose. Add `aria-label="Search"` to the `<button>` element.

findings.title: Icon-only user-menu button has no accessible name
findings.message: Same pattern as the search button above — the `pi-user` icon has no visible text and no `aria-label`. Add `aria-label="User menu"` (or whatever the menu actually does) so assistive tech announces a purpose.

findings.title: Non-descriptive link text "Click here"
findings.message: Out of context — e.g. when a screen reader user pulls a list of links from the page — "Click here" doesn't convey purpose. The surrounding sentence does provide some context, so this is a judgment call rather than a clear blocker. Consider rephrasing so the link reads as e.g. `sign up for an account`.
findings.code_snippet: Don't have an account? Click here to sign up.
